t: Do You Need to Report Money You Have Back Home? | JK Accounting Group Skip to the check JK Accounting GroupTAX · BOOKKEEPING · CFO Book a free call Free check · 2 minutes Do you need to report money you have back home? If you live in the US and still have accounts or money in your home country, the US government may need to know. Not reporting it can be very costly. Check if this is you. By JK Accounting Group · For people with money in two countries Built with JuliaFounder · speaks your language Answer these five questions Just say yes or no. Nothing is saved and no email is needed. 1. Do you have one or more bank or financial accounts outside the US? Yes No 2. This year, did all your accounts outside the US, added together, ever go above $10,000 — even for one day? Yes No 3. Do you also have other money or assets abroad — investments, a pension, or a share of a business? Yes No 4. Can you sign on or control an account outside the US that is not in your name (like a company or family account)? Yes No 5. For US taxes, are you a resident, a green-card holder, or a US citizen? Not sure? Our residency tool can help you check. Yes No Not sure Check if it applies to me Answer all five to see your result. Your result Get this checked — book a free call → A 20‑minute call. No cost, no pressure. Why this matters In plain words — this is one many people never hear about. The US asks about money held abroad. If you are a US taxpayer and your accounts outside the US add up to more than $10,000 at any time in the year, you usually have to report them. It is only a report — you do not pay tax on it. The report simply tells the government the accounts exist. Filing it is straightforward once you know it is needed. Skipping it is expensive. The penalties for not reporting are some of the largest in the tax rules — and almost nobody hears about this until it is too late. That is why it is worth a quick check. Hi, I'm Julia. Founder, JK Accounting GroupEnglish · Russian · Ukrainian Almost everyone who moves to the US still has an account back home. Very few know the US wants to hear about it — and the penalties for staying quiet are harsh. This is exactly the kind of thing we catch for our clients. If it applies to you, we handle the report the right way. Not sure if this is you? Send us your details and we will check it for you — in English, Russian, or Ukrainian. Or book a free 20‑minute call. We tell you clearly what you need to report. We file the reports for you, correctly and on time. A real person who speaks your language. Prefer WhatsApp? Message us here Your name * Email * Phone or WhatsApp Optional. We will use whatever is easiest for you. Best language for you English Russian Ukrainian Send my details Or book a call now Peace of mind, in both countries. We make sure your money back home is reported the right way — no worry, no surprise penalty. Clearly, in your language. Book a free call → See our other free tools Miami · Fort Lauderdale · Online General information, not tax advice. This check is a simple guide. Whether you must file, and which report, depends on your full situation. The $10,000 figure is the FBAR reporting line; other rules and higher limits can also apply. Please talk to us before you act. JK Accounting GroupTAX · BOOKKEEPING · CFO Miami · Fort Lauderdale · Online Bookkeeping · Taxes · CFO — in English, Russian & Ukrainian Book a free call // (function () { "use strict"; var CHECK = ''; var DOT = ''; var NAMES = ["q1", "q2", "q3", "q4", "q5"]; var form = document.getElementById("quiz"); var result = document.getElementById("result"); var hint = document.getElementById("quiz-hint"); function val(n) { var s = form.querySelector('input[name="' + n + '"]:checked'); return s ? s.value : null; } form.addEventListener("submit", function (e) { e.preventDefault(); var answers = {}; var answered = 0; NAMES.forEach(function (n) { var v = val(n); answers[n] = v; if (v) answered++; }); if (answered < NAMES.length) { hint.textContent = "Please answer all five questions."; hint.style.color = "var(--error-text)"; result.hidden = true; return; } hint.style.color = ""; var foreignAccounts = answers.q1 === "yes"; var over10k = answers.q2 === "yes"; var otherAssets = answers.q3 === "yes"; var signature = answers.q4 === "yes"; var usPerson = answers.q5; // yes | no | unsure var hasAccounts = foreignAccounts || signature; var headline = document.getElementById("r-headline"); var explain = document.getElementById("r-explain"); var list = document.getElementById("r-list"); var note = document.getElementById("r-note"); headline.classList.remove("warn"); list.innerHTML = ""; function addItem(cls, icon, text) { var li = document.createElement("li"); li.className = cls; li.innerHTML = icon + "" + text + ""; list.appendChild(li); } // Not a US person → these rules mostly do not apply. if (usPerson === "no") { headline.innerHTML = "This probably does not apply to you yet."; explain.textContent = "These reports are mainly for US taxpayers — residents, green-card holders, and citizens. You told us you are not one for US taxes, so they usually do not apply. This can change the moment your status does."; addItem("ok", CHECK, "You are not a US taxpayer, so US foreign-account reports usually do not apply now."); if (foreignAccounts || otherAssets) addItem("gap", DOT, "You do have money abroad — worth re-checking if your US status changes."); note.innerHTML = "Keep it in mind. The day you become a US resident or citizen, this can start to apply. Not sure about your status? Our residency tool can help — or just ask us."; LM.showResult(result); return; } // US person (yes or unsure) var uncertainPerson = usPerson === "unsure"; if (hasAccounts && over10k) { headline.classList.add("warn"); headline.innerHTML = 'You very likely need to report your foreign accounts.'; explain.textContent = "You have accounts outside the US that went over $10,000 this year. For a US taxpayer, that usually means you must file a report called an FBAR. It is only a report — you do not pay tax on the money — but skipping it carries heavy penalties."; note.innerHTML = "This is important and time-limited. The report has a yearly deadline. We can check your exact case and file it for you, correctly and on time."; } else if (hasAccounts && !over10k) { headline.innerHTML = "You may be close to needing to report."; explain.textContent = "You have accounts outside the US, but you told us they stayed under $10,000 in total. The rule counts the highest point at any moment in the year — so it is easy to cross without noticing."; note.innerHTML = "Worth a quick check. If the total ever touched $10,000 — even for a day — the report is needed. We can help you be sure."; } else if (!hasAccounts && otherAssets) { headline.innerHTML = "You may still need to report assets abroad."; explain.textContent = "You told us you do not have foreign bank accounts, but you do have other assets abroad. Depending on their value, a separate report (Form 8938) can apply to US taxpayers."; note.innerHTML = "Let's confirm. The limits for this one depend on your situation. A quick review tells you if it applies to you."; } else { headline.innerHTML = "You likely have nothing to report here."; explain.textContent = "Based on your answers, you do not have foreign accounts or assets that need one of these reports. If that changes — a new account or money moved abroad — it is worth checking again."; addItem("ok", CHECK, "No foreign accounts or assets that trigger these reports right now."); note.innerHTML = "Good to know. If anything changes, come back and check — or just ask us."; LM.showResult(result); return; } // Build the "what applies to you" list for the reporting cases if (foreignAccounts) addItem("gap", DOT, "You have one or more accounts outside the US."); if (signature) addItem("gap", DOT, "You can control an account abroad that is not in your name — that counts too."); if (over10k) addItem("gap", DOT, "Your foreign accounts went over $10,000 this year."); if (otherAssets) addItem("gap", DOT, "You have other assets abroad — a second report (Form 8938) may also apply."); if (uncertainPerson) addItem("gap", DOT, "You are not sure of your US tax status — worth confirming first (our residency tool can help)."); LM.showResult(result); }); })(); //
